text: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide. [Column](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/column.html). [Technology, Media & Telecom](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/technologie/index.html). ![Julia Krauwer](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/media/Julia_Krauwer_1278x538_tcm16-69446.jpg). 31/01/2023 • 5 minutes read. [Julia Krauwer](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/auteurs/julia-krauwer/index.html). OpenAI’s new chatbot shows an impressive ability to understand and produce language. Yet such technological developments do not reach everyone. Only eight per cent of smaller companies in the Netherlands use artificial intelligence (AI).

text: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide. [Column](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/column.html). [Technology, Media & Telecom](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/technologie/index.html). ![Julia Krauwer](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/media/Julia_Krauwer_1278x538_tcm16-69446.jpg). 31/01/2023 • 5 minutes read. [Julia Krauwer](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/auteurs/julia-krauwer/index.html). OpenAI’s new chatbot shows an impressive ability to understand and produce language. Yet such technological developments do not reach everyone. Only eight per cent of smaller companies in the Netherlands use artificial intelligence (AI). ChatGPT, the underlying AI model of the recently launched chatbot, can handle the most diverse requests.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: OpenAI’s new chatbot shows an impressive ability to understand and produce language. Yet such technological developments do not reach everyone. Only eight per cent of smaller companies in the Netherlands use artificial intelligence (AI). ChatGPT, the underlying AI model of the recently launched chatbot, can handle the most diverse requests. It can explain the theory of relativity in a way a 9-year-old understands, or draft an essay outline on the dangers of the algorithmic filter bubble.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: Yet such technological developments do not reach everyone. Only eight per cent of smaller companies in the Netherlands use artificial intelligence (AI). ChatGPT, the underlying AI model of the recently launched chatbot, can handle the most diverse requests. It can explain the theory of relativity in a way a 9-year-old understands, or draft an essay outline on the dangers of the algorithmic filter bubble. But the chatbot does not shy away from more creative tasks either, such as writing a song lyric or TV dialogue.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: Only eight per cent of smaller companies in the Netherlands use artificial intelligence (AI). ChatGPT, the underlying AI model of the recently launched chatbot, can handle the most diverse requests. It can explain the theory of relativity in a way a 9-year-old understands, or draft an essay outline on the dangers of the algorithmic filter bubble. But the chatbot does not shy away from more creative tasks either, such as writing a song lyric or TV dialogue. For example, the request to write a “hysterically funny TV dialogue” for a couple who are completely fed up with porridge for breakfast produces a genuinely entertaining conversation.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: ChatGPT, the underlying AI model of the recently launched chatbot, can handle the most diverse requests. It can explain the theory of relativity in a way a 9-year-old understands, or draft an essay outline on the dangers of the algorithmic filter bubble. But the chatbot does not shy away from more creative tasks either, such as writing a song lyric or TV dialogue. For example, the request to write a “hysterically funny TV dialogue” for a couple who are completely fed up with porridge for breakfast produces a genuinely entertaining conversation. Behind this impressive model is OpenAI, a research laboratory founded in 2015 with the aim — in the company’s own words — of developing “friendly AI technology”.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: It can explain the theory of relativity in a way a 9-year-old understands, or draft an essay outline on the dangers of the algorithmic filter bubble. But the chatbot does not shy away from more creative tasks either, such as writing a song lyric or TV dialogue. For example, the request to write a “hysterically funny TV dialogue” for a couple who are completely fed up with porridge for breakfast produces a genuinely entertaining conversation. Behind this impressive model is OpenAI, a research laboratory founded in 2015 with the aim — in the company’s own words — of developing “friendly AI technology”. Through safe and transparent solutions, the laboratory hopes to make a positive contribution to society.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: But the chatbot does not shy away from more creative tasks either, such as writing a song lyric or TV dialogue. For example, the request to write a “hysterically funny TV dialogue” for a couple who are completely fed up with porridge for breakfast produces a genuinely entertaining conversation. Behind this impressive model is OpenAI, a research laboratory founded in 2015 with the aim — in the company’s own words — of developing “friendly AI technology”. Through safe and transparent solutions, the laboratory hopes to make a positive contribution to society. OpenAI is connected in several ways to the biggest names in the tech industry.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: For example, the request to write a “hysterically funny TV dialogue” for a couple who are completely fed up with porridge for breakfast produces a genuinely entertaining conversation. Behind this impressive model is OpenAI, a research laboratory founded in 2015 with the aim — in the company’s own words — of developing “friendly AI technology”. Through safe and transparent solutions, the laboratory hopes to make a positive contribution to society. OpenAI is connected in several ways to the biggest names in the tech industry. The organisation was founded with seed capital of no less than 1 billion dollars, from, among others, Tesla boss and OpenAI co-founder Elon Musk, cloud giant Amazon, and venture capital icon Peter Thiel.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: Behind this impressive model is OpenAI, a research laboratory founded in 2015 with the aim — in the company’s own words — of developing “friendly AI technology”. Through safe and transparent solutions, the laboratory hopes to make a positive contribution to society. OpenAI is connected in several ways to the biggest names in the tech industry. The organisation was founded with seed capital of no less than 1 billion dollars, from, among others, Tesla boss and OpenAI co-founder Elon Musk, cloud giant Amazon, and venture capital icon Peter Thiel. Since 2019, Microsoft has also been on board, which is now reportedly considering an [additional investment](https://www.semafor.com/article/01/09/2023/microsoft-eyes-10-billion-bet-on-chatgpt) of ten billion dollars.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: Through safe and transparent solutions, the laboratory hopes to make a positive contribution to society. OpenAI is connected in several ways to the biggest names in the tech industry. The organisation was founded with seed capital of no less than 1 billion dollars, from, among others, Tesla boss and OpenAI co-founder Elon Musk, cloud giant Amazon, and venture capital icon Peter Thiel. Since 2019, Microsoft has also been on board, which is now reportedly considering an [additional investment](https://www.semafor.com/article/01/09/2023/microsoft-eyes-10-billion-bet-on-chatgpt) of ten billion dollars. Struggle to apply AI.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: OpenAI is connected in several ways to the biggest names in the tech industry. The organisation was founded with seed capital of no less than 1 billion dollars, from, among others, Tesla boss and OpenAI co-founder Elon Musk, cloud giant Amazon, and venture capital icon Peter Thiel. Since 2019, Microsoft has also been on board, which is now reportedly considering an [additional investment](https://www.semafor.com/article/01/09/2023/microsoft-eyes-10-billion-bet-on-chatgpt) of ten billion dollars. Struggle to apply AI. Through its investors, the laboratory has been able to build enormous development capacity, both in terms of computing power and scarce AI skills.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: The organisation was founded with seed capital of no less than 1 billion dollars, from, among others, Tesla boss and OpenAI co-founder Elon Musk, cloud giant Amazon, and venture capital icon Peter Thiel. Since 2019, Microsoft has also been on board, which is now reportedly considering an [additional investment](https://www.semafor.com/article/01/09/2023/microsoft-eyes-10-billion-bet-on-chatgpt) of ten billion dollars. Struggle to apply AI. Through its investors, the laboratory has been able to build enormous development capacity, both in terms of computing power and scarce AI skills. This stands in stark contrast to the struggle of the average company to embed such technology in their daily practice.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: Since 2019, Microsoft has also been on board, which is now reportedly considering an [additional investment](https://www.semafor.com/article/01/09/2023/microsoft-eyes-10-billion-bet-on-chatgpt) of ten billion dollars. Struggle to apply AI. Through its investors, the laboratory has been able to build enormous development capacity, both in terms of computing power and scarce AI skills. This stands in stark contrast to the struggle of the average company to embed such technology in their daily practice. In 2020, only 13 per cent of Dutch companies used one or more AI technologies, according to figures from [CBS](https://opendata.cbs.nl/#/CBS/nl/dataset/84830NED/barh?ts=1670493464021).

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: Struggle to apply AI. Through its investors, the laboratory has been able to build enormous development capacity, both in terms of computing power and scarce AI skills. This stands in stark contrast to the struggle of the average company to embed such technology in their daily practice. In 2020, only 13 per cent of Dutch companies used one or more AI technologies, according to figures from [CBS](https://opendata.cbs.nl/#/CBS/nl/dataset/84830NED/barh?ts=1670493464021). Among the smallest companies this share is lower still, namely 8 per cent.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: Through its investors, the laboratory has been able to build enormous development capacity, both in terms of computing power and scarce AI skills. This stands in stark contrast to the struggle of the average company to embed such technology in their daily practice. In 2020, only 13 per cent of Dutch companies used one or more AI technologies, according to figures from [CBS](https://opendata.cbs.nl/#/CBS/nl/dataset/84830NED/barh?ts=1670493464021). Among the smallest companies this share is lower still, namely 8 per cent. Although a divide is emerging here, the major AI developers and the rest of organisations do not live in completely separate worlds.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: This stands in stark contrast to the struggle of the average company to embed such technology in their daily practice. In 2020, only 13 per cent of Dutch companies used one or more AI technologies, according to figures from [CBS](https://opendata.cbs.nl/#/CBS/nl/dataset/84830NED/barh?ts=1670493464021). Among the smallest companies this share is lower still, namely 8 per cent. Although a divide is emerging here, the major AI developers and the rest of organisations do not live in completely separate worlds. Many state-of-the-art AI techniques are shared with the public.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: In 2020, only 13 per cent of Dutch companies used one or more AI technologies, according to figures from [CBS](https://opendata.cbs.nl/#/CBS/nl/dataset/84830NED/barh?ts=1670493464021). Among the smallest companies this share is lower still, namely 8 per cent. Although a divide is emerging here, the major AI developers and the rest of organisations do not live in completely separate worlds. Many state-of-the-art AI techniques are shared with the public. For example, OpenAI’s technology is used in all kinds of [innovative applications](https://medium.com/@theclickreader/openais-gpt-3-powered-applications-list-as-seen-on-twitter-4a3b09b4349) by third parties, such as an AI copywriter or a programme that enables a layperson to draw up annual accounts.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: Among the smallest companies this share is lower still, namely 8 per cent. Although a divide is emerging here, the major AI developers and the rest of organisations do not live in completely separate worlds. Many state-of-the-art AI techniques are shared with the public. For example, OpenAI’s technology is used in all kinds of [innovative applications](https://medium.com/@theclickreader/openais-gpt-3-powered-applications-list-as-seen-on-twitter-4a3b09b4349) by third parties, such as an AI copywriter or a programme that enables a layperson to draw up annual accounts. The way technology companies make this knowledge available does differ.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: Although a divide is emerging here, the major AI developers and the rest of organisations do not live in completely separate worlds. Many state-of-the-art AI techniques are shared with the public. For example, OpenAI’s technology is used in all kinds of [innovative applications](https://medium.com/@theclickreader/openais-gpt-3-powered-applications-list-as-seen-on-twitter-4a3b09b4349) by third parties, such as an AI copywriter or a programme that enables a layperson to draw up annual accounts. The way technology companies make this knowledge available does differ. Sometimes it is a standalone service via an Application Programming Interface (API).

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: Many state-of-the-art AI techniques are shared with the public. For example, OpenAI’s technology is used in all kinds of [innovative applications](https://medium.com/@theclickreader/openais-gpt-3-powered-applications-list-as-seen-on-twitter-4a3b09b4349) by third parties, such as an AI copywriter or a programme that enables a layperson to draw up annual accounts. The way technology companies make this knowledge available does differ. Sometimes it is a standalone service via an Application Programming Interface (API). Other times even the full source code of the model is made public.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: For example, OpenAI’s technology is used in all kinds of [innovative applications](https://medium.com/@theclickreader/openais-gpt-3-powered-applications-list-as-seen-on-twitter-4a3b09b4349) by third parties, such as an AI copywriter or a programme that enables a layperson to draw up annual accounts. The way technology companies make this knowledge available does differ. Sometimes it is a standalone service via an Application Programming Interface (API). Other times even the full source code of the model is made public. Such a truly open-source approach is still the exception, [critics](https://techcrunch.com/2022/05/19/when-big-ai-labs-refuse-to-open-source-their-models-the-community-steps-in/?guccounter=1) in academia note.

AI-as-a-Service.

text: AI-as-a-Service. But while the average scientist benefits from full insight into a model, most entrepreneurs are simply looking for a way to apply AI in an accessible way. AI-as-a-Service, of which ChatGPT is an example, can help. It is AI that is offered, to a greater or lesser extent, ready-made. Many of these services offer a very specific component from the AI toolkit, for example image recognition, translation, or sentiment analysis.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: But while the average scientist benefits from full insight into a model, most entrepreneurs are simply looking for a way to apply AI in an accessible way. AI-as-a-Service, of which ChatGPT is an example, can help. It is AI that is offered, to a greater or lesser extent, ready-made. Many of these services offer a very specific component from the AI toolkit, for example image recognition, translation, or sentiment analysis. Virtual assistants — programmes that can answer customer questions in a chat — are also available as a service.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: AI-as-a-Service, of which ChatGPT is an example, can help. It is AI that is offered, to a greater or lesser extent, ready-made. Many of these services offer a very specific component from the AI toolkit, for example image recognition, translation, or sentiment analysis. Virtual assistants — programmes that can answer customer questions in a chat — are also available as a service. The provider of such assistants supplies the generic technology, after which it can be tailored to the client’s specific case.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: It is AI that is offered, to a greater or lesser extent, ready-made. Many of these services offer a very specific component from the AI toolkit, for example image recognition, translation, or sentiment analysis. Virtual assistants — programmes that can answer customer questions in a chat — are also available as a service. The provider of such assistants supplies the generic technology, after which it can be tailored to the client’s specific case. In addition, there are the so-called machine learning frameworks.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: Many of these services offer a very specific component from the AI toolkit, for example image recognition, translation, or sentiment analysis. Virtual assistants — programmes that can answer customer questions in a chat — are also available as a service. The provider of such assistants supplies the generic technology, after which it can be tailored to the client’s specific case. In addition, there are the so-called machine learning frameworks. These provide the building blocks to create AI models yourself relatively easily, including algorithms that can still be trained on client-owned data.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: Virtual assistants — programmes that can answer customer questions in a chat — are also available as a service. The provider of such assistants supplies the generic technology, after which it can be tailored to the client’s specific case. In addition, there are the so-called machine learning frameworks. These provide the building blocks to create AI models yourself relatively easily, including algorithms that can still be trained on client-owned data. The fact that smaller companies have not yet started experimenting on a large scale is because AI is usually not just a matter of plugging in.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: The provider of such assistants supplies the generic technology, after which it can be tailored to the client’s specific case. In addition, there are the so-called machine learning frameworks. These provide the building blocks to create AI models yourself relatively easily, including algorithms that can still be trained on client-owned data. The fact that smaller companies have not yet started experimenting on a large scale is because AI is usually not just a matter of plugging in. First, technological expertise is essential to identify the possible application areas of AI in a company, and then actually integrate it into operations.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: In addition, there are the so-called machine learning frameworks. These provide the building blocks to create AI models yourself relatively easily, including algorithms that can still be trained on client-owned data. The fact that smaller companies have not yet started experimenting on a large scale is because AI is usually not just a matter of plugging in. First, technological expertise is essential to identify the possible application areas of AI in a company, and then actually integrate it into operations. In a time when IT staff are scarce, that creates challenges.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: These provide the building blocks to create AI models yourself relatively easily, including algorithms that can still be trained on client-owned data. The fact that smaller companies have not yet started experimenting on a large scale is because AI is usually not just a matter of plugging in. First, technological expertise is essential to identify the possible application areas of AI in a company, and then actually integrate it into operations. In a time when IT staff are scarce, that creates challenges. In addition, many AI solutions must first be fed with data from the specific company.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: The fact that smaller companies have not yet started experimenting on a large scale is because AI is usually not just a matter of plugging in. First, technological expertise is essential to identify the possible application areas of AI in a company, and then actually integrate it into operations. In a time when IT staff are scarce, that creates challenges. In addition, many AI solutions must first be fed with data from the specific company. Those data are by no means always available.

Large companies use more AI. Logically, smaller companies in particular suffer from a lack of IT expertise and data.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: In addition, many AI solutions must first be fed with data from the specific company. Those data are by no means always available. Large companies use more AI. Logically, smaller companies in particular suffer from a lack of IT expertise and data. This is also clear when looking more closely at the CBS figures; there is a clear link between company size and AI use.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: Those data are by no means always available. Large companies use more AI. Logically, smaller companies in particular suffer from a lack of IT expertise and data. This is also clear when looking more closely at the CBS figures; there is a clear link between company size and AI use. Of the largest companies, as many as 48 per cent applied AI in 2020 — a big difference from the 8 per cent of the smallest companies.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: Large companies use more AI. Logically, smaller companies in particular suffer from a lack of IT expertise and data. This is also clear when looking more closely at the CBS figures; there is a clear link between company size and AI use. Of the largest companies, as many as 48 per cent applied AI in 2020 — a big difference from the 8 per cent of the smallest companies. However, these figures do not reflect that AI is quietly making its way into all kinds of everyday applications.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: Logically, smaller companies in particular suffer from a lack of IT expertise and data. This is also clear when looking more closely at the CBS figures; there is a clear link between company size and AI use. Of the largest companies, as many as 48 per cent applied AI in 2020 — a big difference from the 8 per cent of the smallest companies. However, these figures do not reflect that AI is quietly making its way into all kinds of everyday applications. During a meeting in Microsoft Teams you can blur or change your background because a facial recognition algorithm ‘sees’ where the surroundings end and the person begins.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: This is also clear when looking more closely at the CBS figures; there is a clear link between company size and AI use. Of the largest companies, as many as 48 per cent applied AI in 2020 — a big difference from the 8 per cent of the smallest companies. However, these figures do not reflect that AI is quietly making its way into all kinds of everyday applications. During a meeting in Microsoft Teams you can blur or change your background because a facial recognition algorithm ‘sees’ where the surroundings end and the person begins. LinkedIn suggests replies in private chats and on posts from people in your network because it uses a model that ‘understands’ and produces language.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: Of the largest companies, as many as 48 per cent applied AI in 2020 — a big difference from the 8 per cent of the smallest companies. However, these figures do not reflect that AI is quietly making its way into all kinds of everyday applications. During a meeting in Microsoft Teams you can blur or change your background because a facial recognition algorithm ‘sees’ where the surroundings end and the person begins. LinkedIn suggests replies in private chats and on posts from people in your network because it uses a model that ‘understands’ and produces language. Newer cars are also packed with AI features, such as automatic parking aids and braking systems.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text:
However, these figures do not reflect that AI is quietly making its way into all kinds of everyday applications. During a meeting in Microsoft Teams you can blur or change your background because a facial recognition algorithm ‘sees’ where the surroundings end and the person begins. LinkedIn suggests replies in private chats and on posts from people in your network because it uses a model that ‘understands’ and produces language. Newer cars are also packed with AI features, such as automatic parking aids and braking systems. To quote the Swedish philosopher Nick Bostrom: “Once something is sufficiently useful and normal, it is no longer called AI.”
Persistent AI divide.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text:
During a meeting in Microsoft Teams you can blur or change your background because a facial recognition algorithm ‘sees’ where the surroundings end and the person begins. LinkedIn suggests replies in private chats and on posts from people in your network because it uses a model that ‘understands’ and produces language. Newer cars are also packed with AI features, such as automatic parking aids and braking systems. To quote the Swedish philosopher Nick Bostrom: “Once something is sufficiently useful and normal, it is no longer called AI.”
Persistent AI divide. OpenAI’s chatbot is of a very different order, and as such symbolises the AI development divide between the tech giants and the rest of the world.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text:
LinkedIn suggests replies in private chats and on posts from people in your network because it uses a model that ‘understands’ and produces language. Newer cars are also packed with AI features, such as automatic parking aids and braking systems. To quote the Swedish philosopher Nick Bostrom: “Once something is sufficiently useful and normal, it is no longer called AI.”
Persistent AI divide. OpenAI’s chatbot is of a very different order, and as such symbolises the AI development divide between the tech giants and the rest of the world. Yet the ubiquity of less spectacular AI — which we therefore do not recognise as such — teaches us that the futuristic technologies of today eventually find their way to the masses.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text:
Newer cars are also packed with AI features, such as automatic parking aids and braking systems. To quote the Swedish philosopher Nick Bostrom: “Once something is sufficiently useful and normal, it is no longer called AI.”
Persistent AI divide. OpenAI’s chatbot is of a very different order, and as such symbolises the AI development divide between the tech giants and the rest of the world. Yet the ubiquity of less spectacular AI — which we therefore do not recognise as such — teaches us that the futuristic technologies of today eventually find their way to the masses. The fact that large-scale experimentation with ChatGPT is now taking place and that people are reflecting on how such technology will change [education](https://www.nu.nl/algemeen/6247374/nieuwe-uitdaging-op-scholen-scholieren-laten-huiswerk-door-chatgpt-maken.html) also points in that direction.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text:
To quote the Swedish philosopher Nick Bostrom: “Once something is sufficiently useful and normal, it is no longer called AI.”
Persistent AI divide. OpenAI’s chatbot is of a very different order, and as such symbolises the AI development divide between the tech giants and the rest of the world. Yet the ubiquity of less spectacular AI — which we therefore do not recognise as such — teaches us that the futuristic technologies of today eventually find their way to the masses. The fact that large-scale experimentation with ChatGPT is now taking place and that people are reflecting on how such technology will change [education](https://www.nu.nl/algemeen/6247374/nieuwe-uitdaging-op-scholen-scholieren-laten-huiswerk-door-chatgpt-maken.html) also points in that direction. There will, however, always be a divide.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: OpenAI’s chatbot is of a very different order, and as such symbolises the AI development divide between the tech giants and the rest of the world. Yet the ubiquity of less spectacular AI — which we therefore do not recognise as such — teaches us that the futuristic technologies of today eventually find their way to the masses. The fact that large-scale experimentation with ChatGPT is now taking place and that people are reflecting on how such technology will change [education](https://www.nu.nl/algemeen/6247374/nieuwe-uitdaging-op-scholen-scholieren-laten-huiswerk-door-chatgpt-maken.html) also points in that direction. There will, however, always be a divide. As soon as one AI technology becomes embedded and is therefore no longer seen as AI, the next spectacular AI model rolls off big tech’s production line.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: Yet the ubiquity of less spectacular AI — which we therefore do not recognise as such — teaches us that the futuristic technologies of today eventually find their way to the masses. The fact that large-scale experimentation with ChatGPT is now taking place and that people are reflecting on how such technology will change [education](https://www.nu.nl/algemeen/6247374/nieuwe-uitdaging-op-scholen-scholieren-laten-huiswerk-door-chatgpt-maken.html) also points in that direction. There will, however, always be a divide. As soon as one AI technology becomes embedded and is therefore no longer seen as AI, the next spectacular AI model rolls off big tech’s production line. If the big tech companies choose to do this open source and thereby share more in-depth knowledge with academia and business, this would at least make the AI divide a little less deep.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: The fact that large-scale experimentation with ChatGPT is now taking place and that people are reflecting on how such technology will change [education](https://www.nu.nl/algemeen/6247374/nieuwe-uitdaging-op-scholen-scholieren-laten-huiswerk-door-chatgpt-maken.html) also points in that direction. There will, however, always be a divide. As soon as one AI technology becomes embedded and is therefore no longer seen as AI, the next spectacular AI model rolls off big tech’s production line. If the big tech companies choose to do this open source and thereby share more in-depth knowledge with academia and business, this would at least make the AI divide a little less deep. The chance that OpenAI will do this seems limited, however.

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide

text: There will, however, always be a divide. As soon as one AI technology becomes embedded and is therefore no longer seen as AI, the next spectacular AI model rolls off big tech’s production line. If the big tech companies choose to do this open source and thereby share more in-depth knowledge with academia and business, this would at least make the AI divide a little less deep. The chance that OpenAI will do this seems limited, however. From the ambition to keep AI “friendly”, the company considers the risk of misuse too great when the source code is available to the entire world.
